'''Anthony Clive Sullivan''' (born 23 November 1968) is a Welsh former professional [[List of dual-code rugby internationals|dual-code international]] [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who played in the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s. He played representative level rugby league (RL) for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] and [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]], and at club level in the [[Rugby Football League Championship|Championship]] for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], and in the [[Rugby Football League Championship|Championship]], and the [[Super League]] for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], as a {{rlp|wg}},<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org (RL)"/> and representative level rugby union (RU) for [[Wales national rugby union team|Wales]], and at club level for [[Cardiff RFC]] (two spells), as a [[Rugby union positions#Wing|wing]].<ref name="Statistics at wru.co.uk (RU)"/> He is the son of [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] (RL) international [[Clive Sullivan]].

==Early life==
Sullivan was born in [[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]], [[East Riding of Yorkshire]], England.<ref name="Profile at cardiffrfc.com (RU)">{{cite web|url=http://www.cardiffrfc.com/Teams/Player?personId=101102|title=Profile at cardiffrfc.com (RU)|publisher=cardiffrfc.com|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}}</ref>

==Playing career==
Sullivan was selected to represent [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] (RL) at the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]]. He played for St Helens on the wing in their [[1996 Challenge Cup]] Final victory over [[Bradford Bulls]].<ref>{{cite web|title=steveprescottfoundation.co.uk|url=http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|work=Steve Prescott Stats|publisher=Steve Prescott Foundation|access-date=3 June 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120808052156/http://www.steveprescottfoundation.co.uk/About/StevePrescottStats.aspx|archive-date=8 August 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref> At the end of [[Super League]]'s first season, he was named on the wing in the 1996 [[Super League Dream Team]]. Together with [[Alan Hunte]] he was the 1997 [[1997 St Helens RFC season|St. Helens]]' top [[Try (rugby)|try]] scorer. In the 1997 post season, Sullivan was selected to play for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] on the wing in the first match of the [[Super League Test series]] against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]]. His usual position was on the wing and he was named in this position in 1998's [[Super League III]] [[Super League Dream Team]]. Sullivan played for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] on the wing in their [[1999 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Bradford Bulls]]. He also played for St Helens on the wing in their [[2000 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]. He played for Wales in the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]]. As [[Super League V]] champions, [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] played against [[2000 NRL season|2000 NRL]] Premiers, [[Brisbane Broncos]] in the [[2001 World Club Challenge]]. Sullivan played on the wing in Saints' victory. He became a dual-code international when he represented the [[Wales national rugby union team]] in 2001.

===County Cup Final appearances===
Anthony Sullivan played {{rlp|lw}} in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' 24–14 victory over [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[1991–92 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]] at [[Wilderspool Stadium]], [[Warrington]], on Sunday 20 October 1991, and played {{rlp|lw}} in the 4–5 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1992–93 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1992–93 Rugby Football League season|1992–93 season]] at [[Knowsley Road]], [[St Helens, Merseyside|St. Helens]], on Sunday 18 October 1992.<ref name="1992-1993 Lancashire Cup Final">{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|title=1992–1993 Lancashire Cup Final|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180713170348/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2745|archive-date=13 July 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref>

===Regal Trophy Final appearances===
Anthony Sullivan played {{rlp|lw}} in [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]' 16–25 defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] in the [[1995–96 League Cup (rugby league)|1995–96 Regal Trophy]] Final during the [[1995–96 Rugby Football League season|1995–96]] at [[Kirklees Stadium|Alfred McAlpine Stadium]], [[Huddersfield]] on Saturday 13 January 1996.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|title=13th January 1996: St Helens 16 Wigan 25 (Regal Trophy Final)|publisher=wigan.rlfans.com|date=31 December 2014|access-date=1 January 2015|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181007005311/http://wigan.rlfans.com/news.php?readmore=2976|archive-date=7 October 2018|url-status=dead}}</ref>
